title: Users report multiple GPU failures in new Steam Machines, reviving the 'red ring of death'
summary: Several units of the new Steam Machine console suffer a catastrophic GPU failure that produces a red line on the screen, reminiscent of the Xbox 360's RROD, leaving the console completely unusable after a few minutes of use.

title: Sony limits sales of PS5 disc drive and industry debates the end of physical format
summary: Faced with a surge in demand after the announcement of a disc-less future, Sony restricts the purchase of the drive to one unit per order, while the VGHF and various companies criticize a decision that accelerates the end of physical games.

title: Intel solves performance issues of its 18A node and prepares new Nova Lake processors
summary: Intel stabilizes production of its 18A node with 30,000 wafers per month, prepares 22-core Core Ultra 5 processors for Nova Lake, and confirms price increases for its Xeon and consumer CPUs.

title: Semiconductor industry urges Trump administration not to intervene in the memory chip market
summary: SK hynix, Samsung, and Micron lead a lobbying group that warns government restrictions on memory supply would worsen the market situation.

title: DRAM and NAND prices will continue to rise through 2026 due to AI data center demand
summary: Despite a slight moderation for consumers, Samsung and other manufacturers forecast increases of up to 20% in the third quarter of 2026 due to high demand from artificial intelligence infrastructure.

title: Japan boosts its technology strategy with robots, Micron's gigafactory, and emergency communications with Starlink
summary: The Asian nation plans to deploy ten million robots to address the worker shortage, Micron opens a $9 billion plant, and hydrants with Starlink are tested for Wi-Fi emergencies.

title: The race for the AI 'superapp' accelerates with Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT Codex, and open source models
summary: Microsoft redesigns Copilot and launches AutoPilot, OpenAI introduces ChatGPT Codex, and the community adds models like Kimi K2.7 to GitHub Copilot, transforming software development while OpenAI's co-founder anticipates a future without interfaces.

title: Mistral launches Leanstral 1.5, an open source model that dominates formal mathematics benchmarks
summary: Mistral's new model demonstrates superior ability in mathematical theorems and proof formalization, being able to detect real errors in software source code.

title: Anthropic developer criticizes Fable 5 development, questioning the future of the video game
summary: A Claude developer shared prompting tips for Fable 5, generating controversy and speculation about development viability and the difficulties facing the franchise's return.

title: Security vulnerability reports have skyrocketed since AI began hunting bugs
summary: A study reveals that the number of reported vulnerabilities exploded coinciding with the use of artificial intelligence in bug hunting, with a notable peak after the launch of Claude Mythos Preview.

title: Google pressures media to give up their content in exchange for revenue from training its AI
summary: Google warns that media outlets that do not allow the use of their articles to train AI models will lose revenue from the News Showcase program, while union negotiations at DeepMind face initial difficulties.

title: Browser competition intensifies with Brave, Safari, and Chrome alternatives
summary: Brave adds Firefox-like containers to isolate accounts, Safari integrates an MCP server for automatic browser debugging, and analysts recommend exploring alternatives given the market's evolution.

title: Multiple critical vulnerabilities: Bad Epoll in the Linux kernel and privilege escalation in MSI Center
summary: The 'Bad Epoll' vulnerability is discovered in the Linux kernel that allows escalating to root on Android systems, while MSI Center exposes a flaw that grants SYSTEM privileges on Windows systems.

title: Data center news: 3D-printed nuclear reactor, canceled megaproject, and Japanese investment
summary: A startup presents the first 3D-printed thorium reactor for AI, Blackstone abandons the world's largest data center campus, and Japan invests $500 million in semiconductors for the future.

title: Weekly cyberattack summary: Avalon ransomware, trojan targeting governments, and North Korean packages
summary: New threats such as Avalon ransomware and the BusySnake trojan target government infrastructures, while North Korea releases 108 malicious packages and flaws are revealed in millions of embedded devices.

title: Immich 3.0, Raycast 0.67, and xplorer² 6.3: productivity software news for advanced users
summary: Immich incorporates non-destructive editing and mobile OCR, Raycast adds content search in files, and xplorer² integrates the Everything search engine to optimize daily work.

title: Apple defends against AI training lawsuit claiming YouTube videos were public
summary: The company responds to the class action lawsuit by stating that the YouTube subtitles used to train its artificial intelligence models were publicly available to everyone.

title: Airbus names and dates the A320 replacement: the eAction plane will take off in 2030
summary: Airbus officially announces its new short-range aircraft, the eAction, whose launch is planned for 2030, marking a milestone in sustainable commercial aviation.

title: Epic Games confirms new free games starting July 9 and Sony announces PS Plus price increase
summary: Epic Games reveals the upcoming games that can be claimed for free on its store, while Sony announces a new increase in the PlayStation Plus subscription.

title: Hollywood will exploit the 'Backrooms' universe and a documentary portrays the biggest box office failure in history
summary: The industry prepares multiple projects on the popular creepypasta 'Backrooms', while a new documentary analyzes the legendary financial flop of a recent blockbuster.
